title: Master Store Manager Dashboard
description: Construct a store management console. You must replace structural div wrappers, create stats layouts, write error-free JS using modern keywords, log array status, and implement interactive elements to add inventory items dynamically.
initialHtml:
Store Manager

Welcome to your Dashboard
changesToBeDone: Replace the container <div> with a <main> element with class 'dashboard'.
Ensure the heading with id 'title' has text 'Store Manager'.
Ensure the subtitle paragraph contains the word 'Dashboard'.
Create exactly 3 statistic cards with class 'stat-card' inside the grid.
Write your JavaScript using only let or const (no 'var' keyword allowed).
Log the text 'Initial count: 3' to the console on page load.
Ensure the console remains free of runtime/compiler errors.
Use the array '.push()' method in your JavaScript file.
Verify that the text input box accepts values correctly.
On clicking 'Add Item', append the new item into the '#item-list' list.

rules.errorMessage: The outer container must not be a <div> element.
The title text must match 'Store Manager' exactly.
The subtitle must contain the word 'Dashboard'.
Expected exactly 3 elements with class 'stat-card' inside 'stats-grid'.
Do not use 'var' keyword. Use 'let' or 'const'.
The console must output the string 'Initial count: 3'.
Correct any runtime script errors in the console.
Your script must use '.push()' to append items to the array.
The text input should register change events correctly.
Clicking the 'Add Item' button must append the new item to the '#item-list'.
